source.description: Built-in image generation created a six-panel pencil process contact sheet for the vintage-roller-skate lesson.
source.notes: The contact sheet was reviewed before cropping; the final panel is used as the finished asset.

process_strategy: The six frames keep one side-view roller skate pose, introducing the boot, sole, wheels, toe stop, laces, ankle padding, and shadow before the final cleanup pass.

final_elements.name: side-view skate boot
final_elements.description: A high-top roller skate boot in side view forms the main silhouette.

final_elements.name: sole plate
final_elements.description: A narrow sole plate sits under the boot and anchors the wheel trucks.

final_elements.name: four roller wheels
final_elements.description: Four rounded wheels sit below the sole plate in two pairs.

final_elements.name: front toe stop
final_elements.description: A small toe stop projects from the front underside of the skate.

final_elements.name: lace holes and laces
final_elements.description: Simple eyelets and crossing laces climb the boot front.

final_elements.name: ankle cuff and stitch details
final_elements.description: A padded ankle cuff, seam lines, and small stitch marks add vintage character.

final_elements.name: soft ground shadow
final_elements.description: A light graphite shadow under the wheels grounds the skate.

frames.visible_job: Place the high-top boot shape with light construction lines.
Draw the narrow sole plate and refine the boot opening.
Add four wheels under the plate and the small front toe stop.
Add eyelets and crossing laces on the boot front without changing the boot silhouette.
Add the padded ankle cuff, seam lines, small stitch marks, and a light ground shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Darken keeper contours, strengthen existing laces and seams, and add restrained graphite shading to the boot, wheels, and shadow.

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
